turns.thought: [OBSERVE] Iteration 616: The swarm has converged completely. Diversity is effectively zero (1.88e-16, normalized 9.27e-19). All particles have the same fitness (1.168e-05) and zero velocity (velocity_zero_ratio = 1.0). No improvement for 200 iterations. The global best is 1.168e-05, very close to the true optimum (0) but stalled. State label indicates premature convergence due to diversity collapse and no movement. | [DIAGNOSE] The swarm has lost all diversity: all particles occupy the same location and have zero velocity. This is classic premature convergence, likely caused by excessive exploitation relative to exploration. The current position is very near the global optimum of Ackley, but because the swarm is a single point, it cannot make the fine adjustments needed to reach zero exactly. The root cause is the complete collapse of the position distribution, preventing any further search. Without intervention, the swarm will remain stuck indefinitely. | [STRATEGIZE] Goal: restore diversity while keeping the near-optimal gbest, allowing the swarm to re-explore the local basin and potentially converge to the exact global optimum (0). A purely randomized reset (reset_worst_particles) could introduce far-away particles and potentially lose the good solution, whereas Levy flight centered around the global best will generate new positions that are mostly near the best but with occasional longer jumps, maintaining proximity while providing the necessary diversity. I will target 20% of particles (since all are equally good/bad) and use a moderate scale and tail parameter to encourage local exploration with some chance of escaping any tiny residual basin. I'll also set inertia weight to 1.0 to allow sustained movement after the perturbation.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The levy_flight intervention did not produce an immediate improvement in best cost. This suggests that the introduced diversity was either insufficient to escape the narrow local basin, or the new positions were not favorable enough to improve the best within one iteration. Given the total collapse of the swarm (all particles identical, zero velocity), a more aggressive re-diversification is needed to break the stagnation. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm remains in a completely stalled state with zero diversity. The levy flight's scale might not have been large enough, or the particles need a broader reset to reinitialize exploration across the search space. The root cause remains premature convergence, and a stronger intervention is required.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The levy_flight and reset_worst_particles interventions failed to improve the best cost because they targeted the worst particles, leaving the global best particle untouched. The global best remains at a near-optimal point (1.168e-05) but cannot refine further because all particles have zero velocity and are identical. The root cause is that the best particle itself is stagnant in a very flat region near the global optimum, requiring a small local perturbation rather than global diversification. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm is not prematurely converged in the sense of being trapped in a poor local optimum; it has found a point extremely close to the true optimum but lacks the momentum and diversity to make the final tiny adjustments. The best particle must be jittered locally to potentially descend into the exact minimum.
